entries.title: COMMENTARY ON JOB 26:1-4 on job 26:1
entries.excerpt: These words mean, "I do not reprove you for defending the role of God or to say the truth that was needed." However, you should not have condemned me, and, in fact, it is possible to plead in favor of God without allowing, at the same time, Job to be exposed to accusations.

entries.title: Commentary on Job on job 26:1
entries.excerpt: Baldath wanted in his last speech to convince Job by the consideration of divine power, terrible to all, in respect of which no man can make a pretense of justice, and innocence so that he asserts that he has been punished without sin. So Jobs give three answers, the first of which is specifically against Baldath, who had tried to frighten Job by the consideration of divine power.
